175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.

196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of

198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.

278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.

284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).

289	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
290	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
291	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
292	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
293	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.

295	   DVRs.

+/-- **Project-side base linear map for `pullback_app_isoTensor`** (iter-185
+Lane F substantive step).
+
+Built from the unit of the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction at the
+`V`-section level: the unit produces a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules
+`N ⟶ (pushforward g).obj ((pullback g).obj N)`, and evaluating its
+underlying `PresheafOfModules`-val at `V` gives a `Γ(X, V)`-linear map
+`Γ(N, V) →ₗ[Γ(X, V)] Γ((pushforward g).obj ((pullback g).obj N), V)`.
+By `pushforward_obj_obj` (definitional), the codomain is the same data as
+`Γ((pullback g).obj N, g ⁻¹ᵁ V)` with `Γ(X, V)` acting through restriction
+of scalars along `g.app V`.

+discharge cleanly via `IsBaseChange.equiv.symm`. -/

+    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (N : X.Modules) (V : X.Opens) :
+    Γ(N, V) →ₗ[Γ(X, V)]
+      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward g).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N), V) :=

+`(g.appLE V U e).hom : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, U)`.

+The codomain of `unitAtV`,
+`Γ((pushforward g).obj ((pullback g).obj N), V)`, is definitionally equal
+to `Γ((pullback g).obj N, g ⁻¹ᵁ V)` by `pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl), which is
+what makes the composition with the presheaf restriction typecheck.

+`pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_isBaseChange` (iter-186 Lane F helper #2). -/

+    letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=

## Changes Made

### File: AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

#### 1) `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` — Σ-pair signature (Stacks 01I8)

**New signature** (verbatim):

```lean
private theorem tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen
    {X : Scheme.{u}} (N : X.Modules) [N.IsQuasicoherent]
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
    letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Spec Γ(X, V), ⊤) :=
      (hV.fromSpec.appLE V ⊤
        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
      Module.compHom _
        (hV.fromSpec.appLE V ⊤
          (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).hom
    Nonempty {iso : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N ≅
        tilde (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V)) //
      ∀ (s : Γ(N, V)),
        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app iso.inv ⊤).hom
            ((tilde.toOpen (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V)) ⊤).hom s) =
          pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
            (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) s}
```

**Carried identity (canonical content)**: `iso.inv` at ⊤-sections sends
`tilde.toOpen Γ(N, V) ⊤ s` to the canonical pullback-section image of `s`
produced by `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap` (the adjunction-unit-based
base map already in scope above). This characterizes the iso as the
inverse of the `fromTildeΓ` counit at the affine open V, exactly
following the docstring marker at L956 "`step1 = (asIso fromTildeΓ).symm`".

The `letI` chain in the signature sets up the Γ(X, V)-Module instance on
the pullback ⊤-section so that `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap`'s
return-type `LinearMap` typechecks inside the Σ-pair.

**Body**: preserved as `exact sorry` (with the same iter-190+ comment
plus an iter-195 marker noting the Σ-pair refactor).

#### 2) `pullback_tildeIso` — Σ-pair signature (Stacks 01HQ)

**New signature** (verbatim):

```lean
private theorem pullback_tildeIso
    {A B : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : A ⟶ B) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    letI : Algebra A B := φ.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra Γ(Spec A, ⊤) Γ(Spec B, ⊤) :=
      ((Spec.map φ).appLE ⊤ ⊤ le_top).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Module Γ(Spec A, ⊤)
        Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M), ⊤) :=
      Module.compHom _ ((Spec.map φ).appLE ⊤ ⊤ le_top).hom
    Nonempty {iso : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
        tilde (ModuleCat.of B (TensorProduct A B M)) //
      ∀ (m : M),
        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app iso.hom ⊤).hom
            (pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap (Spec.map φ) (tilde M) le_top
              ((tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom m)) =
          (tilde.toOpen (ModuleCat.of B (TensorProduct A B M)) ⊤).hom
            (1 ⊗ₜ[A] m)}
```

**Carried identity (canonical content)**: the iso, evaluated at ⊤-sections,
sends the canonical pullback-section image of `tilde.toOpen M ⊤ m` (built
via the `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap` adjunction-unit map applied to
`tilde M`) to `tilde.toOpen … ⊤` applied to `1 ⊗ₜ[A] m`. This characterizes
the iso as the canonical "pullback of tilde = tilde of base change" Spec-level
identification (Stacks 01HQ / 0BJ8).

`le_top` discharges the `⊤ ≤ (Spec.map φ) ⁻¹ᵁ ⊤` obligation since the
preimage of ⊤ is ⊤ (`Opens.map_top : (Opens.map f).obj ⊤ = ⊤` is `rfl`).

**Body**: preserved as `exact sorry` (with the same iter-188+ comment
plus an iter-195 marker noting the Σ-pair refactor).
